name: DUPLICATION CYST
definition: GASTROINTESTINAL DUPLICATION CYST uncommon congenital anomaly found anywhere along mesenteric side of alimentary tract from FOREGUT DUPLICATION long tubular cyst closed at its cranial end, passing through diaphragm in its own hiatus, in intramural gastric cyst lined with secretory epithelium DUPLICATION OF THE HINDGUT double-barreled duplication involving part / all of large bowel with "twin" segment on CYSTIC COLONIC DUPLICATION

demographics.Frequency: 15% of pediatric abdominal masses are gastrointestinal duplication cysts
demographics.Age: presentation often in 1st year of life / early childhood
demographics.Histo: wall of smooth muscle lined with alimentary tract mucosa; ectopic mucosa; squamous,
demographics.Associated with: vertebral anomalies (spina bifida, hemivertebra, fusion defects),
demographics.Location: adjacent to esophagus / within esophageal musculature at any level, paraspinal

dominant_finding: smoothly rounded fluid-filled cyst / tubular structure
distribution: adjacent to esophagus / within esophageal musculature at any level, paraspinal
discriminating_features: smoothly rounded fluid-filled cyst / tubular structure
sharply marginated homogeneous near-water density mass without enhancement

US: elongated tubular / spherical cystic mass with "double-wall / muscular rim" sign:
"muscular rim" sign (= echogenic inner mucosal lining + hypoechoic outer rim) in 47%
sonolucent content with good through transmission ← clear fluid
inner echogenic layer (mucosa) and outer hypoechoic layer (muscle)
cyst with two wall layers: inner echogenic layer of mucosa + outer hypoechoic layer of
low small bowel obstruction ± soft-tissue mass
cyst may serve as lead point for intussusception
mass in concavity of duodenal C-loop
compression + displacement of 1st / 2nd portion of duodenum superiorly + anteriorly
may communicate with pancreatic ductal system through the aberrant duct of an
simultaneous opacification of true + twin colon
air / intestinal matter can enter cyst (20%)
cystic mass: may be echogenic ← solid material ± gas from communication with rectum
spherical cystic lesion that may communicate internally with anorectal lumen / externally
CT: smoothly rounded fluid-filled cyst / tubular structure
sharply marginated homogeneous near-water density mass without enhancement
tubular right posterior mediastinal mass ± air
contrast material may enter through distal connection
paragastric cystic mass up to 12 cm in size, indenting greater curvature
seldom communicates with main gastric lumen at one / both ends
MR: heterogeneous SI of intracystic fluid on T1WI + homogeneous high SI on T2WI
area of high attenuation ← hemorrhage / proteinaceous material
thick enhancing wall / septum + surrounding inflammation
thick-walled closed spherical cyst, almost never communicating
CXR: lobar consolidation + central cavitation ← autodigestion of lung tissue by gastric
displacement of esophagus by paraesophageal mass

clinical.Cx: (1)  Bowel obstruction by intussusception ← cyst at ileocecal junction
